titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 594

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=822 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.772887+00 | translation moa=823 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.782848+00

p.cl:
. W . Rex Scott’ Episcopis . Abbatibus . Prioribus . Comitibus . Iusticiis . Baronibus . Vicecomitibus . Prepositis . Ministris . Et Omnibus hominibus suis francis
et Anglis et Scottis tam presentibus quam futuris . tocius terre sue salutem .

uolo et firmiter precipio . quod nemora Monachorum de
Collingaham . videlicet Grenewde . et totum nemus de Ristuna . et Brocheholewde . et Akesside . et Richedeneswde . et Harewde .
et Denewde . et Swinewde . et Hundewde . et Omnia nemora et Gwastinia sua sint sub defensione Prioris et custodia . necnon et
Monachorum de Collingaham . ne aliquis super . x. libras forisfacture quicquam in predictis nemoribus vel Gwastinis capiat . nisi per ipsum Priorem .
vel per Monachos prenominati loci  si ipse presens non fuerit . Exceptis tantumodo necessariis de Castello meo de Berewich . que
mihimet ipsi solummodo conueniunt . Et si clientes mei pro necessariis meis ad opus Castelli mei de Berewich ad nemora predicta
uenerint  per Priorem uel per clientes suos que opus fuerint mihi et ubi ipse vel clientes sui monstrauerint accipiant . Insuper
addo et illis concedo quod predictus Prior et Monachi habeant Gwarrennam in predictis nemoribus . et per totam terram suam . sicut
extenduntur rectę et Antiquę divise . Videlicet a diuisis inter Berewich et lambertunam usque ad Bilie . et usque
ad Drieforde . et usque ad Riuulum qui manat in mare per Aldechambus pethe . ita quod nullus ibi aliquid capiat . nec
ligna . nec aliqua bestiam siluestrem nisi per ipsos . Quod si aliquis in predictis nemoribus uel Gwastinis capiatur uenenado vel
ligna secando . uel aliquid inde accipiendo . preter licentiam Prioris et monachorum . habeant Prior et monachi quod super illum
et cum illo inuenerint . et ego forisfacturam prenominatam .

Testibus . Ricardo Episcopo sancti Andree . Ingelram Episcopo de Glesgu . Nicholao
Cancellario . Abbate de Ieddewrth . DauiD . olifart iusticio . Dolfino capellano . Hugone clerico regis . Walter de Windleshore .
Robert freburn .

William, king of the Scots, to the bishops, abbots, priors, earls, justices, barons, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all his men, French, English, and Gaelic, as much present as future, of his entire land, greeting.

I will and resolutely instruct that the woods of the monks of Coldingham, viz. Greenwood, and the whole wood of Reston, and Brockholeswood, Aikieside Wood, 'Kirchedeneswde', 'Harewde', 'Denewde', Swinewood, Houndwood, and all their woods and wastelands, be under the protection and keeping both of the prior and monks of Coldingham, so that no one may take anything in the said woods or wastelands, on pain of a £10 fine, unless through the prior himself or the monks of the aforenamed place if he be not present; saving only those things that are necessary for my castle at Berwick, which I alone can agree to; and if my servants come to the aforesaid woods for my needs regarding the work on my castle at Berwick, and where he or his servants have pointed out, they may receive those things I need through the prior or through his servants. In addition, I add and allow to them, that the aforesaid prior and monks may have warren in the aforesaid woods, and throughout their whole land, so far as the right and ancient boundaries extend, viz. from the boundaries between Berwick and Lamberton as far as Billie, and up to 'Drieford', and as far as the stream which flows into the sea by Old Cambus peth, so that no one may take anything there, neither wood, nor any woodland beast, unless by their permission; so that if someone in the aforesaid woods or wasteland is caught hunting or cutting wood, or taking anything from there without the permission of the prior and monks, the prior and monks shall have what is found on him or with him, and I the fine specified before.

As witnesses: Richard, bishop of St Andrews; Ingram, bishop of Glasgow; Nicholas the chancellor; the abbot of Jedburgh; David Oliphant; Dolphin the chaplain; Hugh the king's clerk; Walter of Windsor; Robert Freeburn.
